# **II. Software Requirement Specification** {#ii.-software-requirement-specification}

## **1\. Product Overview** {#1.-product-overview}

The Apartment building System is a software system used to digitize the operation of a small apartment building. The system replaces manual processes such as paper maintenance forms, phone calls to security, direct fee notices, and printed announcements. Residents can submit requests and view information from their mobile phones. Building Staff can manage apartment data, invoices, maintenance requests, visitors, complaints, and announcements from one operational account type. System Admin manages user accounts, roles, master data, and application settings. Release 1.0 focuses on one building with 12 floors. Each floor contains approximately 3 to 4 apartment units, so the system is expected to manage about 36 to 48 apartments. The system is designed for daily resident services and internal building operation management.

![][image2]

Figure 1-1:  Apartment Building Management System overview

As described in the figure 1-2, the system will have the following components

* **Resident**: This component represents the external entity of an apartment occupant. It interacts with the system by sending incoming requests or data such as Service Requests, Fee Payments, and Feedback. In return, it receives outgoing system outputs including Invoices and Notifications.

* **System Admin**: This component represents the administrative user responsible for managing backend operations. It interacts with the system by providing inputs for Configuration / User Management and receives System Reports generated by the core application.

* **Building Staff**: This component represents the internal personnel who handle on-site maintenance and operations. They receive Task Assignments from the system and send back Work Order Updates once tasks are handled. 

* **Payment Gateway**: This external third-party service handles financial processing. The system transmits a Payment Request to it, and the gateway returns the Transaction Result to verify whether the payment succeeded or failed.

## **2\. User Requirements** {#2.-user-requirements}

### **2.1 Actors** {#2.1-actors}

| \# | Actor | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Resident | The individual living in the apartment building. This is the primary and most frequent actor interacting with the system. Residents use the system to pay fees, submit service requests, look up building information, and receive notifications from building management.  |
| 2 | Building Staff | Includes security guards, technicians, cleaning staff, and other building operations personnel. They receive task assignments through the system and update the progress or completion status of their assigned work orders. |
| 3 | System Admin | The individual responsible for the technical operation and maintenance of the system. The admin holds the highest level of access, performing system configuration, user account management, and monitoring overall system reports. |
| 4 | Payment Gateway | A third-party external system responsible for processing financial transactions. This is a non-human actor that serves as the intermediary between the Apartment Building Management System and banks or e-wallet providers, ensuring secure and reliable payment processing. |

### **2.2 Use Cases** {#2.2-use-cases}

#### ***2.2.1 Diagram(s)***

***![][image3]***

#### ***2.2.2 Descriptions***

| ID | Group function | Use Case | Actors | Use Case Description & Main Flow |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 01 | Authentication | Login | Resident | The Resident logs into the system to access resident functions. Main flow: The Resident enters username/email and password → the system validates the login information → if the information is valid, the system redirects the Resident to the main page. |
| 02 | Maintenance Management | View Request Status | Resident | The Resident views the status of submitted maintenance requests. Main flow: The Resident selects View Request Status → the system displays the list of submitted requests → the Resident selects a request → the system displays the current status such as Pending, Confirmed, Processing, or Completed. |
| 03 | Maintenance Management | Submit Maintenance Request | Resident | The Resident submits a maintenance request when there is a problem in the apartment or related building area. Main flow: The Resident enters the issue information, location, and description → the system validates the data → the system saves the request → the request is sent to Building Staff for handling. |
| 04 | Invoice and Payment | Pay Invoice | Resident, Payment Gateway | The Resident pays an invoice through the system. Main flow: The Resident selects Pay Invoice → the system includes View Invoice List to display available invoices → the Resident selects an invoice to pay → the system includes Process Payment → the Payment Gateway processes the transaction → the system updates the invoice status after successful payment. |
| 05 | Invoice and Payment | View Invoice List | Resident | The Resident views the list of invoices assigned to their apartment/account. Main flow: The Resident selects View Invoice List → the system retrieves invoices related to the Resident → the system displays invoice information including fee type, amount, due date, and payment status. |
| 06 | Visitor Management | Register Visitor | Resident | The Resident registers visitor information before the visitor arrives at the building. Main flow: The Resident enters the visitor’s name, phone number, visit time, and purpose of visit → the system saves the visitor information → Building Staff can view and manage the visitor information. |
| 07 | Feedback Management | Feedback | Resident | The Resident sends feedback or suggestions about building services. Main flow: The Resident enters feedback content → the system saves the feedback → the feedback can later be reviewed by Building Staff or System Admin. |
| 08 | Resident Information | View Resident Profile | Building Staff | Building Staff views resident profile information to support building management tasks. Main flow: Building Staff selects View Resident Profile → the system displays resident information such as full name, apartment, phone number, and related details. |
| 09 | Visitor Management | Manage Visitor | Building Staff | Building Staff manages visitor information and visitor entry/exit activities. Main flow: Building Staff selects Manage Visitor → the system includes View Visitor List to display registered visitors → Building Staff can perform Check-in/check-out visitor when the visitor enters or leaves the building. |
| 10 | Visitor Management | View Visitor List | Building Staff | Building Staff views the list of visitors registered by residents. Main flow: Building Staff selects View Visitor List → the system displays visitor name, registered resident, apartment, visit time, and entry/exit status. |
| 11 | Visitor Management | Check-in/check-out visitor | Building Staff | Building Staff confirms when a visitor enters or leaves the building. Main flow: Building Staff selects a visitor from the visitor list → verifies the visitor information → updates the visitor status to Check-in when the visitor arrives or Check-out when the visitor leaves. |
| 12 | Maintenance Management | Manage Maintenance | Building Staff | Building Staff manages maintenance requests submitted by residents. Main flow: Building Staff selects Manage Maintenance → the system includes View Request to display maintenance requests → Building Staff reviews the request details → Building Staff performs Confirm request to approve the request for handling. |
| 13 | Maintenance Management | View Request | Building Staff | Building Staff views the list of maintenance requests submitted by residents. Main flow: Building Staff selects View Request → the system displays request information including sender, apartment, issue description, submission time, and current status. |
| 14 | Maintenance Management | Confirm request | Building Staff | Building Staff confirms whether a maintenance request is valid and ready to be processed. Main flow: Building Staff selects a maintenance request → reviews the request content → confirms the valid request → the system updates the request status to Confirmed or Processing. |
| 15 | Apartment Management | Manage Apartment | System Admin | System Admin manages apartment information in the building. Main flow: System Admin selects Manage Apartment → the system displays the apartment list → System Admin adds, edits, or updates apartment information → the system saves the changes. This use case includes Assign Resident. |
| 16 | Apartment Management | Assign Resident | System Admin | System Admin assigns a resident to a specific apartment. Main flow: System Admin selects an apartment → selects the resident to assign → the system validates the information → the system updates the relationship between the resident and the apartment. |
| 17 | Invoice Management | Manage Invoice | System Admin | System Admin manages resident invoices. Main flow: System Admin selects Manage Invoice → the system displays the invoice list → System Admin creates, updates, or reviews invoice information → the system saves the invoice data. This use case includes Generate Monthly Invoice. |
| 18 | Invoice Management | Generate Monthly Invoice | System Admin | System Admin generates monthly invoices for residents. Main flow: System Admin selects Generate Monthly Invoice → the system retrieves apartment and fee information → the system creates monthly invoices → the invoices are saved so Residents can view and pay them. |
| 19 | User Management | Manage Users | System Admin | System Admin manages user accounts in the system. Main flow: System Admin selects Manage Users → the system displays the user account list → System Admin adds, edits, or updates user information → the system saves the changes. This use case includes Update User Status. |
| 20 | User Management | Update User Status | System Admin | System Admin updates the status of a user account. Main flow: System Admin selects a user account → changes the status to Active, Inactive, or Locked → the system saves the new account status. |
| 21 | Report Management | View Reports | System Admin | System Admin views system reports for management purposes. Main flow: System Admin selects View Reports → the system displays reports related to invoices, payments, residents, apartments, or maintenance → System Admin reviews the report data. |
| 22 | Report Management | Export Report | System Admin | System Admin exports a report to a file. Main flow: System Admin is viewing a report → selects Export Report → the system generates a report file → System Admin downloads the file. This use case extends View Reports because it only happens when the admin wants to export the report. |
| 23 | Payment Processing | Process Payment | Resident, Payment Gateway | The system processes invoice payment through the Payment Gateway. Main flow: The Resident confirms payment → the system sends transaction information to the Payment Gateway → the Payment Gateway processes the payment → the system receives the payment result → the system updates the invoice status. |

## **3\. Software Features** {#3.-software-features}

### **3.1 Functional Overview** {#3.1-functional-overview}

The Apartment Building Management System (ABMS) provides a centralized platform for apartment operations, resident services, financial management, facility booking, complaint handling, and administration.

The system supports six user roles:

* Administrator  
* BQL Manager  
* BQL Staff  
* BQT Head  
* BQT Member  
* Resident

#### ***3.1.1 Screens Flow***

![][image4]

#### ***3.1.2 Screen Descriptions***

*\[Provide the descriptions for the screens in the Screens Flow above\]*

| \# | Feature | Screen | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Authentication | Login | User login using username and password |
| 2 | Authentication | Change Password | Change current password |
| 3 | Apartment Management | Apartment List | View and search apartments |
| 4 | Apartment Management | Apartment Details | View apartment information |
| 5 | Resident Management | Resident List | Manage resident records |
| 6 | Resident Management | Resident Profile | View resident profile |
| 7 | Contract Management | Contract List | View contracts |
| 8 | Contract Management | Contract Details | View contract information |
| 9 | Finance | Invoice List | Manage invoices |
| 10 | Finance | Invoice Details | View invoice details |
| 11 | Finance | Payment History | View payment transactions |
| 12 | Request Management | Request List | View requests and complaints |
| 13 | Request Management | Request Details | Track request processing |
| 14 | Announcement | Announcement List | View announcements |
| 15 | Amenity Booking | Amenity Calendar | Book facilities |
| 16 | Service Management | Service Orders | Manage service orders |
| 17 | Visitor Management | Visitor Registration | Register visitors |
| 18 | Face Authentication | Face Registration | Register facial data |
| 19 | Administration | User Management | Manage users and roles |
| 20 | Administration | System Settings | Configure system parameters |

#### ***3.1.4 Non-Screen Functions***

*\[Provide the descriptions for the non-screen system functions, i.e batch/cron job, service, API, etc.\]*

| \# | Feature | System Function | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Notification | Send Notification Service | Send system notifications |
| 2 | Finance | Automatic Invoice Generation | Generate monthly invoices |
| 3 | Finance | Late Fee Calculation | Calculate overdue penalties |
| 4 | Payment | Payment Verification Service | Verify bank transfers |
| 5 | Face Authentication | Face Matching Service | Compare captured image with registered face |
| 6 | Authentication | Password Reset Service | Send password reset email |
| 7 | Backup | Database Backup Job | Daily database backup |
| 8 | Logging | Audit Logging Service | Record system activities |
| 9 | Reporting | Report Export Service | Export Excel/PDF reports |
| 10 | Visitor Management | Visitor Check-in Service | Process visitor registration |

***3.2.1.2 Use Case Description***

***\<Provide detailed description for  Use case\>***

| Use Case ID |  | UC01 | Use Case Name |  | Login |  |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Author** |  | **HungNV** | **Version** | **1.0** | **Date** | **11/05/2013** |
| **Actor** |  | LMS User |  |  |  |  |
| **Description** |  | The function allows a user to be able to login in the software when he/she have registered an account and his/her account is still active. |  |  |  |  |
| **Precondition** |  | PRE-01:  PRE-02: |  |  |  |  |
| **Trigger** |  | TRG-01: |  |  |  |  |
| **Post-Condition** |  | POS-01: |  |  |  |  |
| **Main flows** |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| ***Step*** | ***Actor*** | ***Action*** |  |  |  |  |
| *1* | User | Type URL into location field of internet browser and then press enter |  |  |  |  |
| *2* | LMS | Display Login screen with the following fields: \- User Name \- Password \- Login and Close button |  |  |  |  |
| *3* | User | Enter user name & password into User Name & Password fields on the Login screen, then click on Login button. |  |  |  |  |
| *4* | LMS | Validate the entered user name & password and then display Home screen |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

**Alternative flows**

| AT1 | At step 2 in the main flows, if there is an internal error in the system, |  |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Sub step** | **Actor** | **Action** |
| 2.1 | LMS | Display "Error" page with message "Internal System Error, please contact with administrator" |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

### **3.3 \<\<Feature Name 2\>\>** {#3.3-<<feature-name-2>>}

## **4\. Non-Functional Requirements** {#4.-non-functional-requirements}

### **4.1 External Interfaces** {#4.1-external-interfaces}

*\[This section provides information to ensure that the system will communicate properly with users and with external hardware or software/system elements.\]*

| Interface |  Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Payment Gateway  | *Connects to a third-party payment provider (e.g., VNPay, MoMo) to process monthly service fee deductions from the household owner’s in-app wallet. The system sends a payment request and receives a transaction result (success or failure).*  |
| Push Notification Service  | *Integrates with Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) or Apple Push Notification service (APNs) to deliver real-time alerts to residents’ mobile devices. Used for billing reminders, payment results, and building announcements.*  |
| Mobile App  | *The resident-facing mobile application (iOS and Android) through which household owners view service fee details, wallet balance, household member information, and payment history.*  |
| Web Admin  | *The browser-based administration interface used by System Admin and Building Staff to manage apartments, residents, vehicles, service fees, and user accounts.*  |

### **4.2 Quality Attributes** {#4.2-quality-attributes}

*\[List all the required system characteristics (quality attributes) specification. Some of the possible attributes are provided with the guide/descriptions are mentioned here\]*

#### ***4.2.1 Usability*** 

*\[This section includes all those requirements that affect usability. For example, specify the required training time for a normal users and a power user to become productive at particular operations specify measurable task times for typical tasks or base the new system’s usability requirements on other systems that the users know and like specify requirement to conform to common usability standards, such as IBM’s CUA standards Microsoft’s GUI standards\]*

The system shall meet the following usability requirements:

\- Admin can add, update, or remove a resident or vehicle record within no more than 3 navigation steps from the main dashboard.

\- The household owner’s home screen shall display wallet balance, current month’s service fee breakdown, and payment status immediately upon login without additional navigation.

\- A new Admin user with basic computer literacy shall be able to complete daily tasks (managing residents, reviewing invoices) without formal training after a self-guided walkthrough of no longer than 30 minutes.

\- The application interface shall support the English language.

\- All destructive actions (deletion of a household or member record) shall require an explicit confirmation dialog before execution.

#### ***4.2.2 Reliability*** 

*\[Requirements for reliability of the system should be specified here. Some suggestions follow:*

*Availability—specify the percentage of time available ( xx.xx%), hours of use, maintenance access, degraded mode operations, and so on.*

*Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) — this is usually specified in hours, but it could also be specified in terms of days, months or years.*

*Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)—how long is the system allowed to be out of operation after it has failed?*

*Accuracy—specifies precision (resolution) and accuracy (by some known standard) that is required in the system’s output.*

*Maximum Bugs or Defect Rate—usually expressed in terms of bugs per thousand lines of code (bugs/KLOC) or bugs per function-point( bugs/function-point).*

*Bugs or Defect Rate—categorized in terms of minor, significant, and critical bugs: the requirement(s) must define what is meant by a “critical” bug; for example, complete loss of data or a complete inability to use certain parts of the system’s functionality.\]*

**1\. Availability**

\- The system shall maintain a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month, equivalent to no more than approximately 3.6 hours of unplanned downtime per month.

\- Scheduled maintenance windows are permitted only between 2:00 AM and 4:00 AM daily. The system must remain fully available outside this window.

\- The 4th and 5th of every month are designated as maintenance-blackout days. No planned downtime is permitted on these dates, as they cover the automated billing cycle.

\- If the Payment Gateway becomes unavailable, the system shall continue operating in degraded mode: residents may still view household information and payment history, and Admin may still manage resident data. Billing transactions will be queued and retried when the gateway recovers.

**2\. Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)**

\- The system shall achieve a minimum MTBF of 720 hours (30 days) between critical failures.

**3\. Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)**

\- Critical failures: the system must be restored within 2 hours of incident detection.

\- Significant failures: must be resolved within 8 hours.

\- Minor failures: may be addressed within the next 3 business days.

**4\. Accuracy**

\- All service fee calculations (electricity, water, sanitation, and parking) must be accurate to zero deviation from the configured pricing formula.

\- Wallet deduction transactions must be fully atomic: a transaction either completes in full or does not execute at all. Partial deductions are not permitted under any circumstances.

\- If a network error or gateway timeout occurs during a payment transaction, the system shall apply the following recovery procedure:

a) Upon reconnection, the system shall automatically query the Payment Gateway to verify the actual transaction status.

b) If the transaction is confirmed as failed, the full deducted amount shall be refunded to the household owner’s wallet within 5 minutes.

c) If the transaction status cannot be determined (e.g., the gateway remains unresponsive), the transaction shall be placed in Pending status and flagged for Admin manual review.

d) In all cases, the household owner shall receive a push notification with the final transaction outcome (Success, Failed, or Pending).

\- Payment status displayed to residents must be synchronized with the actual transaction result within 30 seconds of completion.

**5\. Maximum Bugs / Defect Rate**

\- Overall defect target at release: no more than 5 bugs per KLOC (thousand lines of code).

\- The payment processing and resident management modules must have zero critical bugs at the time of go-live.

**6\. Bug Severity Classification**

| Severity | Definition | Example in this system |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Critical** | Data loss or complete inability to use a core function. Zero tolerance at go-live. | Incorrect fee deducted from wallet; resident data permanently deleted; login completely unavailable. |
| **Significant** | An important feature fails but a workaround exists. | Push notifications not delivered; search returns incorrect results; payment status not updating. |
| **Minor** | Cosmetic or UX issues with no impact on business operations. | Misaligned UI elements; typographical errors in notification text; incorrect font rendering. |

#### ***4.2.3 Performance***

*\[The system’s performance characteristics are outlined in this section. Include specific response times. Where applicable, reference related Use Cases by name.*

*Response time for a transaction (average, maximum)*

*Throughput, for example, transactions per second*

*Capacity, for example, the number of customers or transactions the system can accommodate*

*Resource utilization, such as memory, disk, communications, and so forth.\]*

The system shall satisfy the following performance requirements:

\- The apartment list screen (up to 48 units) shall load and render within 2 seconds under normal network conditions.

\- The automated monthly billing cron job (executed on the 5th of each month) shall complete processing for all households in the building within 5 minutes.

\- The system shall support a minimum of 50 concurrent users (residents and admins) without degradation in response time.

\- Payment gateway API calls shall receive a response (success or failure) within 10 seconds; the system shall handle timeout gracefully and notify the user.

#### ***4.2.4 …***

## **5\. Requirement Appendix** {#5.-requirement-appendix}

*\[Provide business rules, common requirements, or other extra requirements information here\]*

### **5.1 Business Rules** {#5.1-business-rules}

*\[Provide common business rules that you must follow. The information can be provided in the table format as the sample below\]*

| ID | Rule Definition |
| ----- | ----- |
| BR-01 | Each apartment unit has exactly one designated household owner who is responsible for monthly service fee payment.  |
| BR-02 | On the 5th day of each month, the system automatically deducts the total monthly service fee from the household owner’s in-app wallet. |
| BR-03 | If the household owner’s wallet balance is insufficient on the billing date, the household is flagged as Unpaid and the owner receives a push notification.  |
| BR-04 | Monthly service fees comprise electricity, water, sanitation, and vehicle parking charges (if applicable).  |
| BR-5 | Parking fees are calculated based on the number and type of registered vehicles: motorcycles and cars are charged at different rates.  |
| BR-6 | Only System Admin is authorized to add, modify, or delete apartment, household owner, and resident records.  |
| BR-7 | When a household relocates, Admin removes the previous owner’s account and creates a new owner account. All historical invoices of the previous owner are retained in the system for audit purposes.  |
| BR-8 | A household owner may declare additional members to Admin. Admin reviews the declaration and adds the members to the system.  |
| BR-9 | Every resident (including household owner) must have a complete identity record: full name, National ID number, and date of birth.  |
| BR-10 | Vehicle registration for a household can only be updated by Admin upon a formal request from the household owner.  |
| BR-11 | If a payment transaction fails due to a network error or gateway timeout, the system shall verify the transaction status upon reconnection. If the transaction is confirmed as failed, the full amount shall be refunded to the household owner's wallet within 5 minutes. If the status cannot be determined, the transaction shall be placed in Pending status and flagged for Admin review.  |

### **5.2 Common Requirements** {#5.2-common-requirements}

*\[Fill all the common requirements here..\]*

### **5.3 Application Messages List** {#5.3-application-messages-list}

| \# | Message code | Message Type | Context | Content |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | MSG01 | In line | No records match the search criteria  | *No matching records found*    |
| 2 | MSG02 | Push notification | A required input field is left empty  | *The {field\_name} field is required.*   |
| 3 | MSG03 | Toast message | Resident information updated successfully  | *Resident information has been updated successfully.*  |
| 4 | MSG04 | Toast message | New household member added successfully  | *New member has been added to the household*  |
| 5 | MSG05 | Toast message | Monthly service fee deducted successfully  | *Service fee for {month} has been deducted from your wallet successfully.*  |
| 6 | MSG06 | Toast message | Wallet balance insufficient on billing date  | *Wallet balance is insufficient. Please top up to avoid outstanding charges.*  |
| 7 | MSG07 | Toast message | Admin deletes a household record  | *Apartment {apartment\_id} has been removed from the system.* |
| 8 | MSG08 | Confirm dialog | Input value length \> max length  | *You are about to delete apartment {apartment\_id}. This action cannot be undone. Do you wish to continue?*  |
| 9 | MSG09 | Push notification | Monthly service fee deduction failed  | *Your bill for {month} is {amount}. Payment will be automatically processed on the 5th.*  |

### **5.4 Other Requirements…** {#5.4-other-requirements…}
